children.heading: .—Military Musicians Performing in an Official Capacity
children.label: a .—Military Musicians Performing in an Official Capacity

children.text: A military musical unit, and a member of the armed forces who is a member of such a unit performing in an official capacity, may not engage in the performance of music in competition with local civilian musicians.

children.label: i
children.text: is not supported, in whole or in part, by United States Government funds; and

children.label: ii
children.text: is not free to the public.

children.label: i
children.text: is not supported, in whole or in part, by United States Government funds; and

children.label: ii
children.text: is held at a location not on a military installation.

children.label: A
children.text: A performance (including background, dinner, dance, or other social music) at an official United States Government event that is supported, in whole or in part, by United States Government funds.

children.label: i
children.text: is a patriotic event or a celebration of a national holiday; and

children.label: ii
children.text: is free to the public.

children.label: i
children.text: is not supported, in whole or in part, by United States Government funds; or

children.label: ii
children.text: is not free to the public.

children.label: i
children.text: an event that is sponsored by a military welfare society, as defined in ;section 2566 of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: an event that is a traditional military event intended to foster the morale and welfare of members of the armed forces and their families; or

children.label: iii
children.text: an event that is specifically for the benefit or recognition of members of the armed forces, their family members, veterans, civilian employees of the Department of Defense, or former civilian employees of the Department of Defense, to the extent provided in regulations prescribed by the Secretary of Defense.

children.label: i
children.text: to uphold the standing and prestige of the United States with dignitaries and distinguished or prominent persons or groups of the United States or another nation; or

children.label: ii
children.text: in support of fostering and sustaining a cooperative relationship with another nation.

children.heading: .—Prohibition of Military Musicians Accepting Additional Remuneration for Official Performances
children.label: b .—Prohibition of Military Musicians Accepting Additional Remuneration for Official Performances
children.text: A military musical unit, and a member of the armed forces who is a member of such a unit performing in an official capacity, may not receive remuneration for an official performance, other than applicable military pay and allowances.

When authorized under regulations prescribed by the Secretary of Defense for purposes of this section, a military musical unit may produce recordings for distribution to the public, at a cost not to exceed expenses of production and distribution.

Amounts received in payment for a recording distributed to the public under this subsection shall be credited to the appropriation or account providing the funds for the production of the recording. Any amount so credited shall be merged with amounts in the appropriation or account to which credited, and shall be available for the same purposes, and subject to the same conditions and limitations, as amounts in such appropriation or account.

The Secretary concerned may accept contributions of money, personal property, or services on the condition that such money, property, or services be used for the benefit of a military musical unit under the jurisdiction of the Secretary.

Any contribution of money under paragraph (1) shall be credited to the appropriation or account providing the funds for such military musical unit. Any amount so credited shall be merged with amounts in the appropriation or account to which credited, and shall be available for the same purposes, and subject to the same conditions and limitations, as amounts in such appropriation or account.

children.heading: .—Performances at Foreign Locations
children.label: e .—Performances at Foreign Locations
children.text: Subsection (a) does not apply to a performance outside the United States, its commonwealths, or its possessions.

children.heading: .—Military Musical Unit Defined
children.label: f .—Military Musical Unit Defined
children.text: In this section, the term “military musical unit” means a band, ensemble, chorus, or similar musical unit of the armed forces.
